title: nymphaea minor
intro: Nymphaea minor, also known as the lesser duckweed or pygmy water lily, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Nymphaeaceae. It is an aquatic plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Nymphaea minor can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
